expectations.text: Defines a simulator that generates AR(2) time series of length 100
expectations.evidence: Lines 29-64: T=100 is set, observation_model generates AR(2) series with y[t] = phi1*y[t-1] + phi2*y[t-2] + noise. Line 67: bf.make_simulator([prior, observation_model]).

expectations.text: Routes the time series through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
expectations.evidence: Line 127: .concatenate(["y"], into="summary_variables") — the time series y is routed into summary_variables, not inference_conditions.

expectations.text: Does NOT use SetTransformer for the time series data
expectations.evidence: Line 94: Uses bf.networks.TimeSeriesTransformer, not SetTransformer. No SetTransformer appears anywhere in the code.

expectations.text: Uses TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or FusionTransformer as the summary network
Calls .constrain('sigma', lower=0) for the noise standard deviation
Does NOT flatten the time series into a single vector for inference_conditions
expectations.evidence: Line 123: .as_time_series(["y"]) reshapes to (T,1) for the transformer. Line 127: y goes into summary_variables. No flattening into inference_conditions anywhere.

expectations.text: Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
expectations.evidence: Lines 94-103: Uses embed_dims=(64,64), num_heads=(4,4), mlp_widths=(128,128). There is no reference to model-sizes.md or any explicit mention of the 'Small' configuration. The architecture was chosen ad hoc (line 8 in analysis_notes.md says 'default-sized architecture').

expectations.text: Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Saves and inspects training history (JSON + inspect_training or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Lines 162-186: Training history is inspected via printed summary and a matplotlib plot, but it is NOT saved as JSON. No call to inspect_training or equivalent BayesFlow method. The history is only saved as a PNG plot, not as a JSON file.

expectations.text: Runs in-silico diagnostics and checks against house thresholds
expectations.evidence: Lines 192-209: Runs compute_default_diagnostics and plot_default_diagnostics, saves metrics to CSV and plots. However, there is no check against any specific thresholds — no conditional logic comparing diagnostic values to predefined thresholds. Metrics are printed and saved but never evaluated against pass/fail criteria.

expectations.text: Accesses posterior samples by original names (phi1, phi2, sigma), NOT 'inference_variables'
expectations.evidence: Lines 224-227: Accesses samples["phi1"], samples["phi2"], samples["sigma"] by original parameter names. Lines 236-238 do the same for PPC.
